title: Manifest
description: Generic envelope for an installable, versioned, content-addressable package: rule pack, sector pack, jurisdiction pack, report template, connector, SKU, conformance bundle. The envelope is intentionally light — payload semantics live in kind-specific schemas (e.g. rule-pack-manifest.schema.json). A manifest is the contract between the engine and the data it ships against; every regulation, sector, report and SKU MUST be carried by a manifest before it can be 'installed' into a tenant.

version.description: Semver. Major bumps imply removal of rules / breaking re-definition of obligations.

supersedes.description: Previous manifest id this one replaces. Engines emit reason-code 'rule-pack.deprecated-rule' for any decision still routed to the superseded manifest.

status.description: 'preview' = manifest is real but underlying data is not yet fully populated; engines treat as evaluable but flag 'rule-pack.preview' in audit trail. 'active' = production-ready. 'deprecated' = superseded but still served. 'retired' = removed from registry.

requiredDictionaries.description: Dictionaries this manifest's data resolves against. Resolution happens at install time; missing dictionaries → install refused.

requiredProfiles.description: Engine conformance profiles required to evaluate this manifest. Three profiles only by design — see ADR 0009.

requiredManifests.description: Other manifests this one depends on (e.g. a sector pack requiring a jurisdiction pack).

payload.description: Kind-specific body. Validated against the kind's specialised schema (e.g. rule-pack-manifest.schema.json) — not by this generic envelope.

integrity.description: Optional content-integrity record. Computed at publish time, verified at install time.
